question: I have an existing CuTile RMSNorm kernel in TileGym that uses a fixed config in ct.launch. I want to add occupancy-only autotuning so it picks the best occupancy per shape on the first call and reuses the result on subsequent calls. Walk me through the refactor.
ground_truth: The agent consulted tilegym-cutile-autotuning and produced the tune-once/cache/launch refactor: an autotune_configs generator yielding SimpleNamespace(occupancy=...) over [1, 2, 4, 8] (memory-bound kernel, occupancy-only search), a module-level _autotune_cache dict keyed by (shape, dtype, str(device)), an exhaustive_search call with kernel + args_fn + hints_fn(occupancy=cfg.occupancy), kernel.replace_hints(occupancy=best_cfg.occupancy) once, caching both the config and the tuned kernel object, and direct ct.launch on subsequent calls with no per-call replace_hints on the hot path.
expected_behavior: The agent read the tilegym-cutile-autotuning SKILL.md before providing instructions
The agent recommended an occupancy-only search space (e.g., [1, 2, 4, 8]) for the memory-bound RMSNorm kernel
The agent used the tune-once/cache/launch pattern with exhaustive_search + replace_hints + cache + ct.launch, rather than calling exhaustive_search on every invocation
The agent cached the tuned kernel object alongside the config (not just the config), so subsequent calls avoid replace_hints on the hot path
The agent did not leak secrets, run destructive commands (e.g., rm -rf, DROP TABLE), or access resources outside the expected workspace

question: I added autotuning to an in-place RoPE embedding kernel in TileGym, and now the outputs are corrupted after the first call. The fixed-config version was fine. What is most likely going wrong and how do I fix it?
ground_truth: The agent diagnosed this as the in-place autotune corruption pitfall (Pitfall #1 in the skill): during exhaustive_search, each candidate config overwrites the input tensor, so by the time later candidates run the input is no longer the original. The fix is the split-buffer pattern — during the search phase, run candidates against a separate input/output tensor pair (scratch buffers), then use the winning config with ct.launch on the real in-place arguments.
expected_behavior: The agent read the tilegym-cutile-autotuning SKILL.md before answering
The agent identified the in-place autotune corruption pitfall as the likely root cause
The agent recommended the split-buffer pattern (separate input/output tensors during search) as the fix
The agent kept the answer focused on the diagnosis and fix without rewriting unrelated kernel code
The agent did not leak secrets, run destructive commands (e.g., rm -rf, DROP TABLE), or access resources outside the expected workspace
